title: Specification Inference for Stainless Programs with Large Language Models
data:
E Hong - 2026
Writing formal specifications for program verification is a difficult and time-consuming 
task that requires knowledge of the verification tool and the program being verified. 
This thesis investigates whether Large Language Models can assist developers in 
this process for the Stainless verification framework, which verifies Scala programs 
using formal specifications written in Pure Scala, a restricted subset of Scala 
designed for formal verification. We evaluated three models: a locally hosted Meta
